feed.title: Daily Digest
feed.subtitle: Quote, fact, life hack, fortune cookie, and headlines of the day

entry.title: Stock Market Today: Gasoline Prices Fall Below $4
entry.content: Stocks poised to reverse yesterday's slide after Iran deal is signed

entry.title: Tornadoes reported in Illinois, Iowa as severe weather roils Midwest
entry.content: Confirmed tornadoes were reported in Illinois and Iowa Wednesday as severe weather brought the risk of thunderstorms and prompted a flurry of tornado warnings for a large swath of the Midwest​.

entry.title: NASA picks Eric Schmidt’s rocket company for Mars mission, setting up a race with SpaceX
entry.content: Relativity Space—a rocket maker acquired by former Google executive chair Eric Schmidt last year after stumbling on the path to orbit—might just beat SpaceX to Mars.

entry.title: With a sledgehammer and a shovel, volunteers raced to save passengers in Texas plane crash
entry.content: Several motorists who happened across a fiery plane crash on a Texas highway rushed to help — putting their own lives in danger to help those on board escape

entry.title: Tropical Storm Arthur weakens off Texas' Gulf Coast
entry.content: Tropical Storm Arthur, the first named storm of the 2026 Atlantic hurricane season, formed Wednesday off the Gulf Coast of Texas before later weakening to a post-tropical cyclone.

entry.title: Wall Street Hiring Dilemma: AI Can Model—but Can’t Make—the Next Rainmaker
entry.content: Executives are reluctant to slash jobs, with rainmakers often starting out as junior bankers performing rote tasks.

entry.title: How to turn off AI in your Google Docs
entry.content: Here's what you need to do to get those pesky "write with Gemini" pop-ups to go away.

entry.title: South Australia premier says youth social media ban is working, 6 months later
entry.content: Six months after Australia implemented the world's first-ever social media ban for kids under 16, South Australia Premier Peter Malinauskas joins "The Daily Report" to discuss the changes he's seen among teens.

entry.title: Breaking down the powers of a grand jury as DOJ allegedly investigates Gavin Newsom
entry.content: California Gov. Gavin Newsom and his wife are the latest White House adversaries to claim they are now under investigation by the Justice Department. The DOJ has not commented on Newsom's claim. The alleged investigation has raised questions about the powers of a federal grand jury. CBS News legal contributor Jessica Levinson breaks it all down.

entry.title: Breaking down the preliminary U.S.-Iran deal as Trump, Iranian president sign remotely
entry.content: A White House official tells CBS News that President Trump and Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian remotely signed a memorandum of understanding to end the Iran war. Eric Lob, associate professor of politics and international relations at Florida International University, joins with analysis.

entry.title: Fortune Cookie of the Day
entry.content:
If you want to be beautiful, you have to laugh.
Lucky Numbers: 72, 10, 6, 87, 80, 19

entry.title: Life Hack of the Day
entry.content: Expecting mothers can eat bananas between meals to avoid morning sickness.

entry.title: Useless Fact of the Day
entry.content: In York, it is perfectly legal to shoot a Scotsman with a bow and arrow (except on Sundays)

entry.title: Quote of the Day — D. H. Lawrence
entry.content: “But better die than live mechanically a life that is a repetition of repetitions.” — D. H. Lawrence

entry.title: AI is hurting Apple in more ways than one: it may force iPhone price increases
entry.content: CEO Tim Cook said in a recent interview that the situation is "unsustainable."

entry.title: Cargo ship's chief engineer charged in 2024 Francis Scott Key bridge collapse
entry.content: Prosecutors have charged the chief engineer of the cargo ship Dali in connection with the 2024 collapse of Baltimore’s Francis Scott Key Bridge

entry.title: How mistrust and war are fueling a deadly Ebola outbreak
entry.content: Efforts to stop Central Africa's deadly Ebola outbreak face widespread mistrust and porous borders in a war zone.

entry.title: How Trump's memo of understanding with Iran compares to the Obama nuclear deal
entry.content: The Obama-era Iran deal was packed with technical details and specific requirements limiting, but not shutting down, Iran's nuclear program. President Trump's new agreement is more of a framework, and it's not final yet.

entry.title: Trump jokes he'll blame Vance if Iran deal falls through
entry.content: President Trump on Wednesday jokingly said he'll blame Vice President JD Vance if the preliminary deal with Iran falls through. Political strategists Matt Whitlock and Kendra Barkoff Lamy join with analysis.

entry.title: Fed keeps interest rates steady in first decision under Kevin Warsh
entry.content: As was widely expected, the Federal Reserve unanimously voted to keep interest rates unchanged on Wednesday. CBS News coordinating producer Richard Escobedo has more details.

entry.title: Why Trump told DNI pick Jay Clayton to skip Senate hearing
entry.content: The Senate delayed its confirmation hearing Wednesday for director of national intelligence nominee Jay Clayton after Republican Sen. Tom Cotton said President Trump directed Clayton not to appear. CBS News congressional reporter Taurean Small has the latest.

entry.title: When will gas prices return to pre-Iran war levels?
entry.content: Oil prices are falling as the end of the war with Iran seems in sight. Arjun Murti, partner at the energy firm Veriten and the author of the Substack newsletter "Super-Spiked," joins to break down when you can expect the pain at the pump to ease.

entry.title: Veteran speaks out on wife's ICE detention, possible deportation: "It makes me sick to my stomach"
entry.content: ICE recently detained the wife of a retired Army staff sergeant and could deport her, despite her husband's nearly two decades of service to the U.S. CBS News immigration correspondent Camilo Montoya-Galvez has the story.

entry.title: French president urges U.S. to share cutting-edge AI and democracies to cooperate on regulation
entry.content: French President Emmanuel Macron on Wednesday urged the world's wealthy democracies to work together on regulating advanced artificial intelligence systems, speaking at a high-level meeting that included top AI executives.

entry.title: Tim Cook says Apple price increases are 'unavoidable' due to memory crunch
entry.content: RAMaggedon claims another one.

entry.title: Why recess is important for kids
entry.content: You may think school recess is just time for play, but the American Academy of Pediatrics is re-emphasizing that it's important for kids' health. Bradley Blackburn explains why.

entry.title: Rex Heuermann sentenced to life in prison for Gilgo Beach serial killings
entry.content: Rex Heuermann was sentenced to life in prison without the possibility of parole for the series of murders known as the Gilgo Beach serial killings.

entry.title: Fed holds rates steady, pares down statement to remove cutting bias
entry.content: The Federal Reserve on Wednesday released its interest rate decision.

entry.title: This law firm has deep ties to Trump. Now a partner is his pick for a top job.
entry.content: Jamie McDonald, a partner at the firm Sullivan & Cromwell, is President Trump's choice to helm one of the nation's most prestigious federal prosecutors' offices.

entry.title: Federal Reserve holds interest rates steady but leaves door open to hike
entry.content: U.S. inflation is expected to remain elevated through the end of the year, Fed officials say in their latest forecast.

entry.title: All detainees from immigration facility 'Alligator Alcatraz' have been transferred, DHS says
entry.content: Detainees at an immigration detention center in the Florida Everglades, known as “Alligator Alcatraz,” have been transferred to other facilities, the U.S. Department of Homeland Security said, citing concerns related to the hurricane season

entry.title: Brazil's Lula warns Trump not to meddle in Brazil's elections
entry.content: Brazilian President Lula da Silva has warned U.S. President Donald Trump not to interfere in Brazil's upcoming presidential election

entry.title: US in talks with Jamaica to send third-country migrants as rift widens in Caribbean
entry.content: Jamaica is discussing with the United States the acceptance of third-country deportees

entry.title: Man who died in Texas plane crash was a key figure in seeding Austin's technology boom
entry.content: The man who died in this week's crash of a small jet in Texas was a well-known entrepreneur named Joshua Baer who was a successful investor in technology startup companies

entry.title: What Iran gains from preliminary deal with U.S. as details go public
entry.content: Senior U.S. officials dictated the U.S.-Iran memorandum of understanding on a call with reporters Wednesday. CBS News' Nancy Cordes and Samantha Vinograd have the latest.

entry.title: Warsh announces plan to reshape Federal Reserve
entry.content: The Federal Reserve unanimously voted Wednesday to keep interest rates unchanged. It was the first rate decision under the new chair, Kevin Warsh. CBS News MoneyWatch correspondent Kelly O'Grady has the details.

entry.title: Chi-Hua Chien saw Facebook coming — now he says the real AI winners won’t be selling AI
entry.content: Chi-Hua Chien has spent more than two decades as a venture capitalist, but he thinks like a cultural anthropologist.

entry.title: Roelof Botha joins SpaceX’s board of directors
entry.content: The former Sequoia Capital leader is filling an "existing vacancy" on SpaceX's board, days after the company went public in the largest IPO ever.

entry.title: Titan sub: design flaws and company groupthink central to catastrophe, report finds
entry.content: Canadian officials find structural defects in material used for hull and say firm failed to fully test ‘novel’ designCanadian safety officials have issued a damning report on the catastrophic final voyage of the Titan submersible, finding that the US company behind the expedition was overcome by “groupthink” and “confirmation bias” and failed to understand the profound risks confronting their largely untested craft.The 6.7-metre (22ft) carbon fibre submersible dipped below the surface of the Atlantic Ocean in June 2023 en route to the wreckage of the Titanic ocean liner. But nearly two hours after it departed with five passengers, communications went dark. The disappearance prompted a frantic international search, with Canada and the US marshalling all available resources. Continue reading...

entry.title: White House officials unveil details of 14-point plan with Iran
entry.content: Senior White House officials detailed the 14-point memorandum of understanding between the U.S. and Iran following President Donald Trump’s remarks at the G7 summit. NBC News reports on whether the U.S. will allow any nuclear program in Iran of any kind and when military personnel could return home from the region.

entry.title: Trump touts Iran agreement to wrap up G7 summit in France
entry.content: President Trump defended the agreement reached by the U.S. and Iran​ at a press conference to close out the G7 summit in France.

entry.title: Victims ID'd in B-52 bomber crash that killed 8 at Edwards Air Force Base
entry.content: As standard practice, the military makes public identifications 24 hours after next of kin have been notified.

entry.title: Suspect in custody after deadly shooting at Wilmington Hospital
entry.content: A suspect was taken into custody in Philadelphia in connection with the shooting at Wilmington Hospital that left a 19-year-old dead and another injured.

entry.title: Anxious Republicans await details of Iran deal as Trump keeps them guessing
entry.content: Trump is framing a tentative peace deal with Iran as a victory for the U.S., but fractures in the Republican Party suggest that could be a hard sell.

entry.title: Luigi Mangione's lawyers will argue psychiatric defense in NYS murder trial
entry.content: Luigi Mangione's lawyers will argue that he was suffering from an extreme emotional disturbance when he allegedly killed UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson.

entry.title: More united Fed board seen at Warsh's first meeting, according to Kalshi traders
entry.content: Traders on prediction market platform Kalshi largely forecast a unanimous decision among FOMC members at Wednesday's meeting, after April's divided vote.

entry.title: Senate postpones Clayton's confirmation hearing after Trump upends plans
entry.content: The Senate canceled Jay Clayton's confirmation hearing on Wednesday after President Trump's move to delay the installation of the new intelligence chief.

entry.title: Stocks making the biggest moves midday: SpaceX, JPMorgan, UniQure, Intel, Figma, Nano Nuclear & more
entry.content: These are some of the stocks posting the largest moves midday.

entry.title: "No end in sight" as wind and rain batters South, Gulf Coast
entry.content: Louisiana, Texas and Mississippi have been hit with near-constant rain over the past few days. Meteorologist Rob Marciano breaks down the forecast.

entry.title: "The most famous judge in America, for a while," on highs and lows of a Trump trial
entry.content: In an exclusive interview with CBS News, retired Justice Arthur Engoron reflected on the highs and lows of the 2023 Trump civil fraud trial.

entry.title: Trump says Iran deal not final, will resume bombing "if they don't behave"
entry.content: President Trump warns he could order new strikes if Iran's leaders "don't behave," two days before the expected signing of a deal that remains vaguely defined.

entry.title: Donald Trump settles lawsuit against niece over publication of tax records
entry.content: Donald Trump settles lawsuit against niece over publication of tax records

entry.title: Mets oddly linked to AL Cy Young candidate for probable Freddy Peralta reason
entry.content: New York must consider the future beyond 2026.

entry.title: Feyenoord sign Van Bronckhorst as new coach
entry.content: Dutch club Feyenoord Wednesday appointed former captain Giovanni van Bronckhorst as manager, replacing Robin van Persie after a frustrating season that saw them well adrift of rivals PSV Eindhoven.Feyenoord finished second in the Dutch Eredivisie but 19 points behind arch rivals PSV and also endured a tough season in Europe.

entry.title: WNBA will increase regular season to 50 games, starting in 2027
entry.content: NEW YORK -- The WNBA continues to grow.

entry.title: Miles Russell figuring out that 'boring' golf can lead to U.S. Open success
entry.content: Miles Russell, the 17-year-old amateur who is playing in his first major championship at the U.S. Open, strives for "boring golf" at Shinnecock.

entry.title: Novig Promo Code TSNEWS50: Get $50 World Cup Bonus for England-Croatia, Any Other Match
entry.content: Learn how to sign up with the Novig promo code TSNEWS50 and score $50 in Novig Coins for World Cup trades.

entry.title: ESPN responds to Fox World Cup coverage beef, touts ‘comprehensive coverage’ despite rights restrictions
entry.content: ESPN is covering the World Cup with the same fervor it would for any other major global sporting event, despite draconian restrictions levied by FIFA on broadcasters that do not own the rights to air the tournament. One glance at a press release issued by ESPN prior to the World Cup would suggest the network…

entry.title: Dana White ‘Can’t Afford’ Another White House UFC Event
entry.content: The recently held “UFC Freedom 250” bout on the South Lawn of the White House lived up to the hype The Dana White ‘Can’t Afford’ Another White House UFC Event first appeared on The Blast

entry.title: Which Rangers Were Overpaid, Underpaid in 2025-26, including Miller, Gavrikov
entry.content: The New York Rangers have no shortage of big-money contracts on their roster, but a new NHL salary model suggests […]

entry.title: Fed Chair Warsh expected to withhold 'dot' from central bank's interest rate outlook
entry.content: The central bank's Federal Open Market Committee is set to release its quarterly update of where individual officials expect interest rates to head.

entry.title: CME Group's Terry Duffy to step down in 2027, CFO Lynne Fitzpatrick to become CEO
entry.content: Duffy, 67, will transition to executive chairman effective March 1, 2027, the company said Wednesday.

entry.title: 'I would have dropped Vaibhav Sooryavanshi': Sanjay Manjrekar slams teenager after on-field clash
entry.content: Former India batter Sanjay Manjrekar suggested dropping Vaibhav Sooryavanshi from the India A squad as a disciplinary action following an on-field altercation with Sri Lanka A players. Despite provocations, Manjrekar emphasized that physical contact is unacceptable. The 15-year-old's recent form has also been inconsistent, contrasting with his stellar IPL 2026 performance.

entry.title: Vikings Reacts Survey: What Are We Worrying About?
entry.content: There are areas of concern

entry.title: U.S. Open 2026: Jackson Koivun leads the 20 amateurs competing this week at Shinnecock Hills
entry.content: The top amateur in the world is set to turn pro after the U.S. Open.

entry.title: Rich Eisen a fan of Dolphins rebuild so far
entry.content: Eisen thinks Dolphins are making right decisions this offseason.

entry.title: State leaders honor Corning-Painted Post track star Cella McLaughlin
entry.content: State officials honored Corning-Painted Post senior Cella McLaughlin for her impressive state track titles.

entry.title: Roman Anthony injury update: Red Sox outfielder has six-week, follow-up MRI
entry.content: Red Sox outfielder Roman Anthony underwent a follow-up MRI on his injured hand and it came back clean.

entry.title: Chargers Daily Links: Bolts start minicamp with strong attendance
entry.content: Your source for all Chargers and NFL news from around the web.

entry.title: Local Wrestler helps guide Pennsylvania 16U National Team to history
entry.content: Rob Shay has been coaching his 14-year-old son, RJ, for the last eight years. Rob Shay has seen his son produce at some of the highest levels, with finishes in the top three in both state and national tournaments. But RJ Shay seemed to hit another level during the 16U National Duals in Farmington, Utah, from June 10-13. The plight of a December birthday pitted 14-year-old RJ Shay against the ...

entry.title: 1 killed when small airplane crashes on Texas highway. People leave vehicles to try to help
entry.content: A business jet with six people on board crashed on a highway in Laredo, Texas, and caught fire Tuesday night, authorities said, killing one person and causing chaos as people left their vehicles to frantically try to smash the cockpit window and free those inside.

entry.title: Goodyear Blimp turns 101 with first-ever music festival inside airship hangar
entry.content: The guest list included sports mascots, Care Bears, a DJ and, naturally, a blimp.

entry.title: Scott Laughton looking at raise after trade from Maple Leafs to Kings
entry.content: The free agent center should have lots of suitors.

entry.title: Portugal vs. DR Congo live score, result, highlights from 2026 World Cup Group K match
entry.content: The Sporting News is tracking live scoring updates and highlights for Portugal vs. DR Congo in the 2026 FIFA World Cup. Follow for complete results from the Group K match in Houston.

entry.title: Jason Dickinson $20 million contract update impacts Oilers
entry.content: Dickinson was a member of the Chicago Blackhawks before being traded.

entry.title: Most goals in World Cup history: Where Messi, Mbappe and more rank among FIFA's top scorers all time
entry.content: Here's a list of the players with their names on the scoresheet the most times in World Cup history.

entry.title: World Cup 2026 standings: Live updated table, points for every group in FIFA tournament
entry.content: Sporting News is tracking live World Cup standings and points for every group.

entry.title: Manchester United pull out of race for Nottingham Forest star
entry.content: It's starting to look like Old Trafford won't host Adam Wharton next season.

entry.title: ‘Addiction is proof there is a devil. Recovery is proof there is a God’: Irish rockers Bleech 9:3 on struggle, sobriety and their stunning debut
entry.content: After two friends sponsored each other in Alcoholics Anonymous, they started making music. As they gear up for a summer of 40 festivals, the band tell their harrowing yet uplifting storyOn stage in a Camden pub, Barry Quinlan, frontman of Irish rockers Bleech 9:3, shares the intensity of Joy Division’s Ian Curtis. He hunches and jerks around the mic stand and his eyes bore a hole in the back wall as jubilant teenagers expand and contract in a circle pit. The gig in mid-May has the same I-was-there energy as early Arctic Monkeys or Fontaines DC shows; with major labels signing Bleech 9:3 on both sides of the Atlantic, dozens of festival dates this summer and a wildly impressive, impassioned five-song debut EP, the band will soon be playing much bigger rooms than this.But when I meet Barry and his three bandmates earlier on that day, there’s none of that twitchy energy. Bleech 9:3 bring calm to a meeting room in their management company’s offices as staff bustle around outside. That stillness is hard-earned: Barry and guitarist Sam Duffy are each other’s sponsor for Alcoholics Anonymous (AA). Quinlan smiles: “It’s an anonymous programme, so we’ll say ‘alleged sponsor’.” Continue reading...

entry.title: AI executives gather at G7 as Europeans seek checks on American dominance
entry.content: Artificial intelligence takes center stage Wednesday at the G7 meeting in France

entry.title: African and Commonwealth nations in Kenya urge quick execution of a key treaty protecting oceans
entry.content: African and Commonwealth nations have called for a swift implementation of a landmark treaty protecting the high seas

entry.title: Sierra Leone’s first lady refuses to condemn FGM without ‘reliable data’ on harms
entry.content: Exclusive: health professionals, survivors and politicians voice concerns in open letter over comments by Fatima Maada Bio, who denies supporting the practiceThe first lady of Sierra Leone has denied that she supports female genital mutilation amid rising anger around her perceived approval of the practice.But in an exclusive response to the Guardian, Fatima Maada Bio, the wife of President Julius Maada Bio, also said she would not openly condemn FGM until she saw “reliable data” that the practice was harmful. Continue reading...

entry.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
entry.content: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

entry.title: Potential first tropical cyclone of the year developing in the Gulf
entry.content: Forecasters have issued an advisory for the potential first tropical cyclone of the 2026 hurricane season, warning it could develop into a tropical storm by Wednesday.

entry.title: Early results put Lewis George in lead to win Democratic nomination for D.C. mayor
entry.content: The District's progressive candidates took healthy leads in crucial primary races Tuesday night as a new voting system resulted in hourslong delays.

entry.title: Iran says deal with U.S. requires Israeli forces to leave Lebanon
entry.content: Iran's foreign minister says Israeli troops can't remain in Lebanon under the pending deal with the U.S.

entry.title: Detainees moved out of "Alligator Alcatraz" over hurricane concerns, ICE says
entry.content: ICE has moved detainees out of a controversial soft-sided detention center in the Florida Everglades known as "Alligator Alcatraz," a spokesperson said, citing safety concerns around hurricane season.

entry.title: Trump-backed Barry Moore projected to win runoff in Alabama GOP Senate primary
entry.content: Rep. Barry Moore won the Republican Senate runoff in Alabama on Tuesday night, CBS News projects, defeating political newcomer and U.S. Navy Seal Jared Hudson.

entry.title: Rick Jackson wins Republican nomination for governor in Georgia
entry.content: Rick Jackson wins Republican nomination for governor in Georgia

entry.title: Trump-backed Rep. Mike Collins projected to win Georgia GOP Senate primary runoff
entry.content: Rep. Mike Collins will face off against Democratic Sen. Jon Ossoff in November as Republicans look to Georgia to deliver a key GOP victory that could determine control of the Senate.

entry.title: Latest U.S. strike on alleged drug boat kills 1, leaves 2 survivors, Pentagon says
entry.content: Critics have questioned the overall legality of the boat strikes as well as their effectiveness since the U.S. military began them in September 2025.

entry.title: Argentina is defending its World Cup title, and its fans are more obsessed than ever
entry.content: Some Argentina fans shelled out tens of thousands of dollars to follow the World Cup defending champions around the U.S. One group made a 20-hour drive to Tuesday’s opening match in Kansas City against Algeria, living on sandwiches, to save money

entry.title: Fortune Cookie of the Day
entry.content:
The wise man is the one that makes you think that he is dumb.
Lucky Numbers: 36, 27, 56, 24, 48, 38

entry.title: Life Hack of the Day
entry.content: Add half a cup of lemon juice to your laundry to brighten whites.

entry.title: Useless Fact of the Day
entry.content: The average chocolate bar has 8 insects' legs in it.

entry.title: Quote of the Day — Robert Greene
entry.content: “Never whine, never complain, never try to justify yourself.” — Robert Greene

entry.title: Trump administration uses hydrogen peroxide against algae in Reflecting Pool
entry.content: President Donald Trump's remodeled Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool has turned chartreuse from an algal bloom

entry.title: The Dell XPS 13 laptop is available starting today
entry.content: The Dell XPS 13 is now available, starting at $599.

entry.title: What should Sixers do with Dominick Barlow's team option for 2026-27?
entry.content: One has to wonder what the Philadelphia 76ers should do with Dominick Barlow's team option for the 2026-27 season.

entry.title: Keba Keita on what he can bring to a team: 'I'm an effort, energy guy, it doesn't matter what team I'm on'
entry.content: Keba Keita on what he can bring to a team: 'I'm an effort, energy guy, it doesn't matter what team I'm on'
